events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need parse scenario. Field service visit, PPE selection, Safety & Rigging. Observed condition: arc-flash rating below task category (label vs. clothing - DOC+ID). Work rough-complete. Need identify component or condition and visible defect cues. Decision fail because dangerous/non-compliant. Risk high/critical? Arc flash PPE insufficient is critical maybe high. Need component_conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. percent_complete rough-complete maybe 80. value? Not numeric reading asked. Could be 0. findings: PPE clothing arc rating less than incident energy/category on label; worker not protected for task. actions: replace with properly rated arc flash PPE, verify ATPV/cal/cm2 matches label, stop work. references: NFPA 70E 130.7(C), OSHA 1910.269/l 1910.335? Actually OSHA 1910 Subpart I PPE. NFPA 70E. Need JSON shape. Ensure no prose outside.

events.label: response

{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",

  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 80,
  "value": 0,

  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Arc-flash PPE clothing rating is below the task category/incident energy shown on the equipment label.",
    "Documented PPE selection does not match the identified arc-flash hazard category.",
    "Worker would be under-protected during energized work or troubleshooting."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work on or near exposed energized conductors until proper arc-rated PPE is provided.",
    "Select and don arc-flash PPE with an ATPV or arc rating equal to or greater than the label/category requirement.",
    "Verify PPE selection against the site arc-flash hazard analysis and OEM service information.",
    "Retrain personnel on NFPA 70E PPE category/incident-energy matching."
  ],
  "rationale": "The observed PPE has a lower arc-flash rating than the task category indicated by the equipment label and documentation, creating a serious shock/arc-flash injury exposure. This is non-compliant and must be corrected before work proceeds.",
  "references": [
    "NFPA 70E, Article 130.7(C) - Personal Protective Equipment",
    "NFPA 70E, Table 130.7(C)(15)(a) and (b) - Arc-Flash PPE Categories",
    "OSHA 1910 Subpart I - Personal Protective Equipment"
  ]
}
